scenario: Catalyst Banking runs Camunda 8.8 for a loan platform handling 3500 loans per day. A risk operator wants to delete an obsolete decision definition version from Operate, but a colleague warns that the deletion will affect more than just the chosen decision. The team wants to know which DRD-level impacts the userguide documents for this action.
question: Which set of impacts does the Decisions-page delete-resources flow attribute to deleting a decision definition?

options.text: Only the chosen decision table is removed from the DRD, while literal expressions inside the same DRD remain available so existing process incidents on the cluster can still resolve against them
Only the metadata header of the chosen decision is removed, while the underlying DMN XML is kept on the cluster and the DRD continues to evaluate from the previous version of the definition

The DRD that contains the chosen decision is deleted, including all decision tables and literal expressions in that DRD, and deleting the only existing version of a decision definition could result in process incidents
The DRD is deleted only if it has been deployed less than 30 days ago, after which a new evaluation against the same bpmnProcessId silently uses the previous version of the decision instead

a.text: Incorrect. The userguide states that deleting a decision definition removes the whole DRD that contains it, including all other decision tables and literal expressions in that DRD, not only the chosen decision.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no documented metadata-only deletion that keeps DMN XML on the cluster; the note states the DRD is deleted entirely along with its tables and literal expressions.

c.text: Correct. The note on Delete decision definition from Decisions Page states that deleting a decision definition deletes the DRD, including all decision tables and literal expressions that are part of the DRD, and warns that deleting the only existing version of a decision definition could result in process incidents.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs do not gate deletion on a 30 days deployment age, and they do not describe a silent fallback to the previous version of the decision after deletion.

explanation: Delete decision definition from Decisions Page warns that deleting a decision definition deletes the DRD that contains it, removing all other decision tables and literal expressions in that DRD. The note also flags that deleting the only existing version of a decision definition could result in process incidents in Operate.
